sources.title: 1979 - Black Jack - by Ken Loach
sources.description:
When honest young Tolly is forced on the run with 'Black Jack,' adventure and mishap are never far away. Based on Leon Garfield's popular novel, Ken Loach's 'Black Jack' is a dark, complex adventure film set in the 18th century.

Director: Ken Loach
Writers: Leon Garfield, Ken Loach
Stars: Stephen Hirst, Louise Cooper, Jean Franval

Genres: Adventure, Comedy

metadata.Plot: A rediscovered classic from director Ken Loach (THE WIND THAT SHAKES THE BARLEY, KES) and one of the inspirations for Wes Anderson's MOONRISE KINGDOM, BLACK JACK is a dark and complex adventure film set in the 18th century and based on Leon Garfield's novel for children. When French thief "Black Jack" kidnaps young Tolly, a journey begins that includes traveling fairs, murder and the rescue of a young girl from a private insane asylum. While shot primarily in 16mm and on a modest budget on location in Yorkshire, Chris Menges' photography, the enchanting performances by its young cast and Loach's gentle, observational style all shine through.
metadata.Actors: Stephen Hirst, Louise Cooper, Jean Franval
